entities.name: ASIAN TROPICS
entities.description: Hotspots of climate change effects and associated weather variabilities in the tropics of Asia

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Global phenomenon causing weather variabilities and extremes

entities.name: WEATHER VARIABILITIES
entities.description: Uncertainty and inter-annual variability in weather conditions

entities.name: WEATHER EXTREMES
entities.description: Severe weather events that crops are frequently exposed to within a single season

entities.name: RESILIENCE
entities.description: Inbuilt ability of crop varieties to withstand variable weather conditions

entities.name: TOLERANCE
entities.description: Ability of a crop to withstand individual stresses in a specific situation or stage

entities.name: C4 CROPS
entities.description: Crops characterized by a specific carbon fixation pathway known for wider adaptation to climatic conditions

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: A C4 crop targeted for the development of stress-resilient germplasm

entities.name: RESEARCH FOR DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: Projects focusing on saving achievable yields across variable environments

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced, which the program aims to maintain under optimal and variable conditions

entities.name: ABIOTIC STRESSES
entities.description: Non-living environmental factors that negatively impact crop growth

entities.name: BIOTIC STRESSES
entities.description: Living organisms, such as pests and diseases, that negatively impact crop growth

entities.name: GENOMICS
entities.description: Novel breeding tool used to develop stress-tolerant maize germplasm

entities.name: DOUBLE HAPLOID TECHNOLOGY
entities.description: Novel breeding tool used to accelerate the development of homozygous maize lines

entities.name: PRECISION PHENOTYPING
entities.description: Novel breeding tool used for accurate measurement of plant traits

entities.name: GENOTYPE X ENVIRONMENT INTERACTION
entities.description: The way different genotypes respond to different environments, which the program seeks to reduce

entities.name: MULTIPLE STRESS TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of maize germplasm to grow well across various variable weather conditions and stresses

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: A sub-optimal growing condition characterized by water deficiency

entities.name: HEAT
entities.description: A sub-optimal growing condition characterized by high temperatures

entities.name: WATERLOGGING
entities.description: A sub-optimal growing condition characterized by soil saturation

entities.name: VIRULENT DISEASES
entities.description: Highly infectious biotic stresses that affect maize
